description: Faced with inflation and labor shortages, the Japanese government pushed for a significant increase in the national minimum wage in 2022. This challenged the traditional 'lifetime employment' model.

key_events.description: Prime Minister Kishida calls for wage growth to tackle inflation.
Keidanren (business lobby) expresses caution over small-business impact.
Central Minimum Wage Council recommends a record-high average increase.
Prefectures finalize their specific minimum wage hikes.

calibration_notes: Good for calibrating public sentiment when policy is driven by macroeconomic necessity rather than grassroots activism.
